name: Bill Mays
abstract: William Allen Mays (born February 5, 1944), known professionally as Bill Mays, is an American jazz pianist from Sacramento, California.

interviews.title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

concerts.label: Music from Japan: 25th Anniversary Celebration of Japanese Orchestral Music/Honoring the 40th Anniversary of the Tokyo-New York Sister-City Relationship
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Mays, the wonderful Bill Mays was there. It was always basically Gerry, piano, bass and drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: the two recordings with Bill I really enjoy, you know just the two of us playing
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Magnusson
about_quotes.evidence: I had Bill Mays, who I had been friends with for years, knew him from San Diego
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Magnusson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: So I went out there. It was Gene Perla, Jan Hammer, and myself for a while. Then a little down the line, it got to be Willie Mays – everybody used to laugh when she’d say Willie Mays. So he changed his name to Bill Mays.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
